title: 破镜谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\137-破镜谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Miss Marple explains the motive behind the death of Heather Badcock to Jason Radcliffe and Detective Constable Caddle. She reveals that Heather Badcock had previously contracted measles and, despite being ill, attended a celebration to meet the movie star Marina Gregg to obtain her autograph. During that encounter, Heather unknowingly infected the pregnant Marina Gregg with measles. This infection caused Marina's child to be born with mental and physical defects, a tragedy that haunted Marina for years.

Miss Marple asserts that when Heather Badcock met Marina Gregg again and boasted about her bravery in attending the event while sick, Marina acted impulsively. Marina had previously ingested camphor, a dangerous drug, into her own drink. To ensure Heather consumed it, Marina intentionally pushed Heather's arm, causing Heather to spill her own cocktail. Marina then insisted that Heather drink from her own cup after cleaning the mess. To cover her tracks, Marina attempted to frame herself as the intended victim by staging threatening letters and self-poisoning incidents.

Miss Marple suggests that Jason Radcliffe had known the truth about the measles all along and had been attempting to protect Marina from further harm or legal consequences. Following the explanation, Detective Inspector Tidler interrupts the conversation. Miss Marple then visits Marina Gregg, who lies dead on a bed. Miss Marple muses whether Marina died from a self-administered overdose of pills or if someone else provided them, while Jason Radcliffe reflects on Marina's beauty and suffering.
